---
title: FAERS
alias: &ALIAS faers
href: https://www.fda.gov/drugs/surveillance/fda-adverse-event-reporting-system-faers
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The FDA Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS) is a pharmacovigilance database containing adverse event and medication error reports submitted to FDA, used for post-marketing safety surveillance.

## Key Features

- Contains millions of post-marketing adverse event and medication error reports
- Publicly available as quarterly data extracts in ASCII and XML formats
- Supports disproportionality analyses such as reporting odds ratios (ROR) and PRR calculations
- Links adverse events to drug names, doses, indications, and patient demographics
- Integrated into OpenFDA API for programmatic pharmacovigilance signal detection

## Use Cases in Research

- Detecting pharmacovigilance signals for adverse events associated with antimicrobial drugs
- Characterizing adverse event profiles of novel antibiotics post-approval
- Identifying drug-drug interaction signals in patients receiving combination antibiotic therapy
- Analyzing adverse event reporting biases and patterns using data science methods
- Supporting real-world evidence studies on antibiotic safety and tolerability

## Getting Started

Visit [FAERS](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/surveillance/fda-adverse-event-reporting-system-faers) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
